Canonical target architecture source for WBS22 Wave 6 schema/table/manual cleanup and field migration planning. Use this packet before any Wave 6 schema-sensitive action. Source basis: operator approved Wave 6 target-architecture approach on 2026-05-07; live Airtable schema readback table universe; WBS20 sequencing and approval model; WBS21 decomposition/readback rules; Wave 6 planning notes. Rule: exact field/table changes still require their own approval packet.

Target architecture principles:
1. Use current live schema readback as the starting inventory every time.
2. Classify every live table before Wave 6 execution as included, excluded, deferred, or WBS23 scaffold-only.
3. Treat status/state/stage/scope/type/role/authority/retention/gate/family fields as controlled select candidates when they contain bounded vocabularies.
4. Treat dates and timestamps as date/dateTime, ranks/counts as numbers, yes/no gates as checkboxes, stable human keys as single-line text, evidence/notes/rationale as multiline text.
5. Add computed/formula/lookup/rollup fields only when they reduce manual inconsistency or create safe readback signals; computed fields must not replace human approval decisions.
6. Prefer new typed replacement field plus backfill plus validation over direct field-type conversion.
7. Preserve old fields until migration validation and dependency review are complete; old-field deletion is a separate approved schema/delete decision.
8. Use Delete Queue for record deletion workflow only; do not use it for table/schema deletion.
9. Defer scaffold lifecycle/disposition to WBS23 unless Wave 6 only needs non-disposition schema readback.

Table-by-table target architecture classification:
- Work Items: review for controlled select normalization, review_after/retention consistency, plan linkage, and optional computed review/readiness helpers.
- Session Checkpoints: review checkpoint_status, trigger, github_promotion_status, retention_class, review_after, checkpoint_at, and continuity/searchability fields.
- Idea Inbox: review status, related_area, retention_class, review_after, captured_at, and promotion fields.
- Plans: review plan_state, retention_class, review_after, active task fields, Queue Control links, and resume/readiness fields.
- Operator Preferences: review status, scope, retention_class, curation_decision, review_after, and preference-key searchability.
- Validation Test Cases: review Area, Execution Lane, Observed Status, Active, retention_class, review_after, and test-key design.
- Queue Control: core authority surface; schema changes require separate high-safety approval. Review only for controlled fields and schema consistency.
- Gemini Research Reference: reference surface; review only if research table architecture is explicitly scoped.
- Governance Control Plane: core authority surface; no schema change unless separately approved after dependency review.
- Repo Surface Registry: review surface_type, authority_status, retention_class, review_after, locator/key/searchability fields.
- dcoir-memory-preflight: review invocation_mode, task_family, status, retention_class, review_after, and helper-memory searchability.
- dcoir-decision-policy: review decision_family, status, retention_class, review_after, and decision-rule searchability.
- dcoir-validation-orchestrator: review validation_scope, status, retention_class, review_after, and validation-memory searchability.
- Delete Queue: review approved_to_delete, delete_stage, retention_class, target_table/target_record_id identity fields, timestamps, and verification fields. No processing in Wave 6.
- Validation Evidence: review result, retention_class, review_after, evidence key, source locator, and evidence summary structure.
- Admin Registry: review object_type, status, retention_class, review_after, object/table governance fields, and last_reviewed_at.
- DCOIR Lifecycle Ledger: review lifecycle_object_type, lifecycle_stage, migration_status, retention_class, event_at, payload_json, and source identity fields.
- Local Configuration Registry: review config_kind, status, sensitive/safe/confirmed checkboxes, runtime reference fields, and no-secret rule.
- Operator Tools Registry: review tool_family, status, active, retention_class, review_after, validation timestamp, and launcher/source fields.
- DCOIR Cleanup WBS: review level, surface, state, gate, target, rank, review_after, validation_notes, and context.
- DCOIR Cleanup Scaffold Registry: defer to WBS23 for disposition; Wave 6 may only classify/read schema if needed for non-disposition architecture awareness.

Wave 6 finalization rule: do not execute schema/table/field changes, direct type conversion, select option changes, computed field creation, value backfill, reference updates, or field/table deletion until exact target table/field/action, before snapshot, dependency review, explicit approval, and after-readback plan exist.
